frameworks.name: maximum entropy models
frameworks.one_line: statistical-physics model fit to simultaneous spiking activity of neural populations

frameworks.name: Ising-like models of neural population
frameworks.one_line: pairwise spin-glass description of correlated neural firing patterns

frameworks.name: statistical mechanics of neural codes
frameworks.one_line: treating population activity as a thermodynamic system with collective states

core_question: Can simple statistical-physics models quantitatively capture the joint activity patterns of large real neural populations?
key_assumptions: population activity is well-described by low-order interaction models
neurons recorded together can be treated as a statistical ensemble
model adequacy depends on how cells are sampled from tissue
manipulations: sampling geometry: dense local samples vs sparse samples from larger regions
population size scaled to 1000+ simultaneously recorded neurons
key_findings: dense samples from a small region yield extremely detailed quantitative agreement between theory and data
sparse samples from larger regions cause the models to fail
simple statistical-physics models can match population activity beyond qualitative resemblance
interpretations: one can aspire to precise quantitative agreement between simplifying theory and complex biological activity
sampling structure fundamentally constrains the validity of population-level statistical-physics descriptions
limitations_noted: models break down under sparse sampling across larger spatial regions
